statement: This record remains negative evidence on build 26100: the repo did not produce enough supporting proof to promote it into a normal actionable surface.

anticheat_risk.anticheat_details: Changing virtualization and VBS posture can affect kernel integrity surfaces and compatibility with some anti-cheat stacks.

gating_reason: Static routing is promising, but the path-aware ETW lane stayed no-hit and the family still carries a nearby VBS collision in winload.exe.

links.title: Repo security notes for EnableVirtualization
links.summary: Repo security notes document EnableVirtualization and its value meanings under the Policies\System path.

links.title: Win25H2Clean 96-key phase-0 existence batch
links.summary: The 96-key existence batch confirmed EnableVirtualization exists on the clean baseline with observed value 1.

links.title: Boot-phase UAC policy cluster lead
links.summary: The repo boot-phase source mirror references EnableLUA, EnableVirtualization, and EnableInstallerDetection under Policies\System during boot-phase processing.

links.title: Path-aware static probe for EnableVirtualization
links.summary: The path-aware static pass found an exact current-build ntoskrnl.exe value hit and kept EnableVirtualization, EnableLUA, and EnableInstallerDetection in the same naturally resolved function. The nearby VBS collision remained isolated to winload.exe.

links.title: Path-aware lightweight ETW follow-up for EnableVirtualization
links.summary: The tools-hardened path-aware ETW lane completed shell-safe and produced real ETL/CSV outputs, but still did not capture an exact runtime read or intended-path line for EnableVirtualization.

links.title: Secondary-profile path-aware ETW replay for EnableVirtualization
links.summary: The secondary-profile path-aware ETW replay completed shell-safe on Win25H2Clean-B and reproduced the same clean no-hit for the exact value name and intended path.
